tax_note: GST registration required above ₹20L turnover; presumptive taxation for small businesses under ₹50L

vat_gst.name: GST
vat_gst.registration_threshold: ₹20 lakh annual turnover (₹10 lakh for special category states)
vat_gst.registration_threshold_note: Mandatory above threshold. Below threshold, voluntary registration available. Composition scheme available for businesses under ₹1.5 crore (lower rate, simplified filing, but cannot sell interstate or on e-commerce platforms).
vat_gst.digital_products: Digital products and SaaS are classified as OIDAR (Online Information and Database Access or Retrieval) services, taxed at 18% GST. Non-resident sellers providing OIDAR services to Indian consumers must register for GST regardless of threshold.

digital_product_tax: SaaS products, templates, courses, and digital downloads are taxed at 18% GST under OIDAR services. If selling to overseas customers, exports of services are zero-rated (0% GST) provided payment is received in convertible foreign exchange. This makes export-focused digital businesses tax-efficient.

entity_comparison.setup_cost: ₹0 (GST registration is free; shop license varies by state)
entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Income taxed at personal slab rates (5-30%). Presumptive taxation under Section 44ADA: pay tax on 50% of gross receipts if total receipts under ₹50L.
Flat 30% tax on profits + 4% health and education cess. No DDT on distributions.
entity_comparison.best_for: Professional services; two or more partners; simpler compliance than Pvt Ltd

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: 25% corporate tax (for companies with turnover under ₹400 crore) + 4% cess. Dividend taxed in hands of shareholders at slab rates.
entity_comparison.best_for: Seeking investors; revenue above ₹50L; international clients prefer dealing with a company

isp_note: Jio Fiber / Airtel Xstream widely available; 4G fallback everywhere
